event: View of a Moscow square with Orthodox churches
symbols: onion domes — the flame of prayer rising heavenward in Orthodox tradition
multiple churches clustered together — the density of sacred space in a devout city
empty square — civic life ordered around the axis of worship
description: A watercolor depicting a wide, open square in Moscow flanked by low arcade buildings on the right and several Orthodox churches on the left and center. The churches display characteristic onion domes — one gilded, another dark, and a tall blue spire rising behind them. A round colonnaded structure appears at the far left edge. The sky is pale grey-blue, and the square is largely empty with a few small figures. Handwritten inscriptions appear at the top and bottom margins of the sheet.
